provenance.gitCommitNote: trainer/tooling code commit for this task's two new bins (wthor_to_simple, egaroucid_filter_stones); no changes to t090_distillation.rs/train_data.rs. Report/meta committed separately per repository commit discipline.

countReconciliation.splitSchemeDifference: train_patterns_v3 splits by game (last 10% of games -> frozen holdout, game-count basis, exactly 90.0%/10.0%); t090_distillation.rs simple-corpus mode splits by individual record via fnv1a(canonicalKey)%100 (0-89 train / 90-94 validation / 95-99 frozen-discarded). Both are ~90% train by construction but not numerically identical because one is per-game and the other is per-position-hash.

egaroucidFilterStones.note: 4-13 stone counts match the README population table cited in T153 exactly (e.g. 13 stones = 434029). Measured total (1,514,097) is ~84k above the task's rough pre-registered estimate (~1.43M) because both the 14- and 15-stone buckets are capped at the full 500,000.

trainerChange.note: t090_distillation.rs and train_data.rs are byte-for-byte unchanged from T153; both new tools only produce plain-text files consumed by the existing, unmodified --simple-corpus path.

cargoTest.result: PASS (99 passed; 0 failed) - 90 pre-existing tests unchanged and green + 9 new tests (5 wthor_to_simple, 4 egaroucid_filter_stones)

comparisonToTrainPatternsV3_T124.note: Same 4,431,504 WTHOR samples, same v4 pattern set, same teacher signal; only the trainer differs (t090_distillation.rs simple-corpus vs train_patterns_v3). +0.39 regret gap isolates the trainer as the dominant source of the T153 Egaroucid-vs-WTHOR confound.

comparisonToRunA.note: Same trainer, same pool size (4,431,504) as Run A; B's regret (1.2333) is lower (better) than A's (1.5000) by 0.2667. Point-estimate data-quality signal, secondary per this task's pre-registered interpretation since the trainer gap (Run A vs T124) dominates.

Run C (mixed) beats Run A (WTHOR-only) by 0.0667 regret but falls short of Run B (Egaroucid-only matched pool, 1.2333); plausibly because C is 74.5% WTHOR by sample count (4,431,504 of 5,945,601) so the Egaroucid quality contribution is diluted, and because the Egaroucid stones<=15 subset skews toward opening-phase positions rather than covering all game phases the way B's full reservoir sample does.

interpretationApplied.preRegisteredBranch: (2): A is clearly worse (1.5000, in the 1.5-1.9 band) than the known 1.1111 point, so the t090_distillation.rs trainer itself (not the data) is judged the dominant gap source; B and C's absolute values are treated as reference-only, not evidence for scaling t090_distillation.rs + Egaroucid to full size.
interpretationApplied.relativeOrderingWithinTrainer: B (1.2333) < C (1.4333) < A (1.5000): Egaroucid data does not underperform WTHOR under this trainer at matched/added scale, but this is a secondary signal per the applied branch.
interpretationApplied.recommendedNextStep: Out of scope for this task: investigate what specifically differs between t090_distillation.rs's simple-corpus teacher-only training and train_patterns_v3 on identical data (loss shape, LR schedule, regularization, phase-stage computation), and/or bring good external data into train_patterns_v3's own pipeline instead of t090_distillation.rs.

scopeNote: This task covers a diagnostic same-trainer probe only (WTHOR-simple-conversion tooling, Egaroucid stone-count filter tooling, three matched-scale trainings, oracle comparison). Full 25.5M-record training, game-play gating, train_patterns_v3 code changes, and production adoption are explicitly out of scope.
